refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable the operator (on-site or remote) to execute earthmoving tasks with measurable productivity of ≥ productivity_target (m³/h or ton/h) during representative operations over the agreed evaluation period, while maintaining compliance with worksite safety plan and operational policies.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-001 is a high-level acceptance criterion that conflates productivity measurement with operational capability. Refinement separates the measurement/verification aspect from the capability delivery aspect. The refined FR explicitly acknowledges dual operator modes (on-site and tele-operated), incorporates the safety and policy constraints as boundary conditions, and defines the measurable outcome (m³/h or ton/h) as the verification target rather than the functional requirement itself.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Conduct representative operations trials over the agreed evaluation period; measure actual productivity (volume moved per hour or tonnage per hour); compare against productivity_target; document compliance with worksite safety plan and operational policies via safety audit and policy checklist.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The Vehicle shall execute earthmoving tasks (dig, load, transport, dump) with mechanical efficiency and control responsive to operator commands.

FR-NGHE-001.2: The Teleoperation system shall transmit operator intent (commands) to the Vehicle and return real-time telemetry (position, load, status) with latency and fidelity sufficient for safe remote operation.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The Safety Services shall monitor operational state and enforce safety constraints (speed limits, geofencing, collision avoidance, emergency stop) to prevent unsafe conditions.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The NGHE Solution shall integrate Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services such that productivity targets are achievable without violating safety or policy constraints.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Equip Vehicle with high-power hydraulic actuators and load-sensing control; optimize cycle time via operator training and task scheduling; measure productivity via onboard sensors and post-mission analytics.

DP-B: Implement dual-mode operation (on-site manual + tele-operated); use real-time telemetry feedback and predictive task planning to maximize throughput; enforce safety constraints via Safety Services to prevent productivity loss due to safety violations.

DP-C: Integrate Vehicle mechanical efficiency (DP-A) with Teleoperation fidelity (DP-B) and Safety Services constraint enforcement (DP-C); use closed-loop feedback from productivity KPI measurement to tune operator interface and task allocation.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-C: Integrate Vehicle mechanical efficiency, Teleoperation fidelity, and Safety Services constraint enforcement via closed-loop feedback from productivity KPI measurement to enable dual-mode operation (on-site and tele-operated) that achieves productivity_target while maintaining safety and policy compliance.

potential_sources: Vehicle mechanical design (DP-A) is independent of Teleoperation latency/fidelity (DP-B); however, both must be tuned together to achieve productivity_target without violating safety constraints.
Safety Services constraint enforcement (DP-C) may reduce peak productivity if constraints are overly conservative; coupling risk exists between safety margin and productivity target.
Operator training and task scheduling (implicit in DP-A) must be coordinated with Teleoperation interface design (DP-B) to avoid mode-switching delays.
coupling_check.notes: DP-C is a system-level integration pattern (closed-loop feedback) rather than a single design parameter; it couples three independent sub-DPs. Coupling is acceptable at this level because the FR itself is a system-level acceptance criterion. Decoupling will occur at the next decomposition level when sub-FRs are mapped to sub-DPs.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Productivity_target is achievable within the mechanical and operational constraints of the Vehicle design.
Assumption: Teleoperation latency and fidelity can be tuned to support remote operation without unacceptable productivity loss.
Assumption: Safety Services constraints can be enforced without creating deadlock or mode-switching delays that prevent productivity_target achievement.
Risk: If Vehicle mechanical efficiency is fundamentally limited (e.g., hydraulic power insufficient), no amount of Teleoperation or Safety Services tuning will achieve productivity_target.
Risk: If Teleoperation latency exceeds operator reaction time, remote operation may be unsafe or inefficient, forcing reliance on on-site operation only.
Risk: If Safety Services constraints are too conservative, they may prevent productivity_target achievement; if too permissive, they may allow unsafe conditions.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: DP-C is appropriate for this level. At the next decomposition level, decompose DP-C into three independent sub-DPs (one for each sub-FR) to enable parallel design and reduce coupling. Explicitly define the closed-loop feedback mechanism (measurement, analysis, tuning) as a separate DP at a lower level.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution is the top-level system boundary that integrates Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. It owns the closed-loop feedback mechanism for productivity KPI measurement and tuning. It is responsible for ensuring that the integrated system achieves productivity_target while maintaining safety and policy compliance.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives task definitions (dig location, load target, dump location, cycle time target) from Site Manager or task planning system.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Transmits real-time operational telemetry (Vehicle position, load, status, productivity metrics) to Site Manager and Tele-operator for monitoring and feedback.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Transmits safety alerts and constraint violations (speed limit exceeded, geofence breach, collision risk, emergency stop) to Safety Manager and operator.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Provides communication channel for Teleoperation commands, telemetry feedback, and safety alerts between on-site/remote operator and Vehicle.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the top-level system boundary; it owns the integration of Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. The closed-loop feedback mechanism for productivity KPI measurement and tuning is contained within NGHE Solution. Minimal diffusion: only external interfaces (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Network Link) cross the boundary.

Vehicle owns mechanical efficiency (DP-A) but does not own Teleoperation fidelity (DP-B) or Safety Services constraint enforcement (DP-C). Productivity_target achievement requires integration with Teleoperation and Safety Services, which are external to Vehicle. High diffusion: Vehicle must interface with Teleoperation (commands, telemetry) and Safety Services (constraint enforcement).

Teleoperation owns fidelity and latency (DP-B) but does not own Vehicle mechanical efficiency (DP-A) or Safety Services constraint enforcement (DP-C). Productivity_target achievement requires integration with Vehicle and Safety Services. High diffusion: Teleoperation must interface with Vehicle (commands, telemetry) and Safety Services (constraint enforcement).

Safety Services owns constraint enforcement (DP-C) but does not own Vehicle mechanical efficiency (DP-A) or Teleoperation fidelity (DP-B). Productivity_target achievement requires integration with Vehicle and Teleoperation. High diffusion: Safety Services must interface with Vehicle (constraint enforcement) and Teleoperation (constraint enforcement on commands).

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module boundary is the appropriate IM for CR-NGHE-001 because it owns the system-level integration and closed-loop feedback mechanism required to achieve productivity_target while maintaining safety and policy compliance. The sub-modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) are contained within NGHE Solution and are responsible for delivering their respective sub-DPs (mechanical efficiency, teleoperation fidelity, constraint enforcement). The NGHE Solution integrates these sub-modules and measures productivity KPI to enable closed-loop tuning.
containment_risks: Risk: If the closed-loop feedback mechanism (measurement, analysis, tuning) is not explicitly owned by NGHE Solution, it may be diffused across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services, leading to inconsistent or delayed tuning.
Risk: If NGHE Solution does not have clear authority over task allocation and mode switching (on-site vs. tele-operated), productivity optimization may be suboptimal.
Risk: If the interfaces between Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services are not well-defined and owned by NGHE Solution, integration delays and rework may prevent productivity_target achievement.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: NGHE Solution is the correct IM boundary for CR-NGHE-001. At the next decomposition level, assign sub-FRs to sub-modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) and define the interfaces between them explicitly. Ensure that the closed-loop feedback mechanism is implemented as a separate module or service within NGHE Solution (e.g., Productivity Analytics, Task Optimizer) to avoid diffusion.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-C (integrated Vehicle efficiency, Teleoperation fidelity, and Safety Services constraint enforcement via closed-loop feedback) directly addresses the refined FR (enable operator to execute earthmoving tasks with measurable productivity ≥ productivity_target while maintaining safety and policy compliance). The DP is sufficient to satisfy the FR at the system level. No independence axiom violation: the DP is a single, coherent integration pattern that does not impose unnecessary constraints on sub-DPs.

diffusion_risks: Risk: The closed-loop feedback mechanism (measurement, analysis, tuning) must be explicitly owned by NGHE Solution; if diffused across sub-modules, it will create inconsistent or delayed tuning.
Risk: The interfaces between Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services must be well-defined and owned by NGHE Solution; if not, integration delays and rework may occur.
Risk: Task allocation and mode switching (on-site vs. tele-operated) must be owned by NGHE Solution; if diffused to sub-modules, productivity optimization may be suboptimal.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the appropriate IM boundary with high containment score (95) and minimal diffusion (5). The sub-modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) are contained within NGHE Solution and are responsible for delivering their respective sub-DPs. The NGHE Solution integrates these sub-modules and owns the closed-loop feedback mechanism.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of CR-NGHE-001 is feasible via representative operations trials: measure actual productivity (m³/h or ton/h) over the agreed evaluation period; compare against productivity_target; audit compliance with worksite safety plan and operational policies. NGHE Solution is the appropriate boundary for verification because it owns the integration and measurement of productivity KPI.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The refined FR is allocated to NGHE Solution, which is decomposed into sub-FRs allocated to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. The allocation is clear and traceable. Each sub-module is responsible for delivering its sub-DP, and NGHE Solution is responsible for integrating them and measuring productivity KPI.

recommended_revisions: At the next decomposition level, explicitly define the closed-loop feedback mechanism (measurement, analysis, tuning) as a separate DP and assign it to a dedicated module or service within NGHE Solution (e.g., Productivity Analytics, Task Optimizer).
Define the interfaces between Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services explicitly, including data flow, timing, and error handling.
Define the task allocation and mode switching logic (on-site vs. tele-operated) explicitly and assign ownership to NGHE Solution.
Establish acceptance criteria for each sub-FR and sub-DP to enable verification at lower levels of decomposition.

decision.justification: CR-NGHE-001 is a system-level acceptance criterion that requires decomposition into sub-FRs and sub-DPs to enable detailed design and verification. The refined FR is not atomic; it encompasses multiple independent concerns (Vehicle mechanical efficiency, Teleoperation fidelity, Safety Services constraint enforcement, and closed-loop feedback). The selected DP (DP-C) is a system-level integration pattern that couples three independent sub-DPs. The assigned IM (NGHE Solution) is the appropriate top-level boundary, but it must be decomposed into sub-modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) with explicit sub-FRs and sub-DPs. Proceeding to the next decomposition level is necessary to enable parallel design, reduce coupling, and establish clear ownership and interfaces.
decision.justification_detail: The refined FR has four sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-001.1 through FR-NGHE-001.4) that are independent and can be designed in parallel. The selected DP (DP-C) is a system-level integration pattern that couples three independent sub-DPs (Vehicle mechanical efficiency, Teleoperation fidelity, Safety Services constraint enforcement). The assigned IM (NGHE Solution) is the appropriate top-level boundary, but it must be decomposed into sub-modules with explicit ownership and interfaces. Proceeding to the next decomposition level will enable detailed design of each sub-module and explicit definition of the closed-loop feedback mechanism.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The Vehicle shall execute earthmoving tasks (dig, load, transport, dump) with mechanical efficiency and control responsive to operator commands.

FR-NGHE-001.2: The Teleoperation system shall transmit operator intent (commands) to the Vehicle and return real-time telemetry (position, load, status) with latency and fidelity sufficient for safe remote operation.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The Safety Services shall monitor operational state and enforce safety constraints (speed limits, geofencing, collision avoidance, emergency stop) to prevent unsafe conditions.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The NGHE Solution shall integrate Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services such that productivity targets are achievable without violating safety or policy constraints.

assumptions: Productivity_target is achievable within the mechanical and operational constraints of the Vehicle design.
Teleoperation latency and fidelity can be tuned to support remote operation without unacceptable productivity loss.
Safety Services constraints can be enforced without creating deadlock or mode-switching delays that prevent productivity_target achievement.
Operator training and task scheduling are coordinated with Teleoperation interface design to avoid mode-switching delays.
The closed-loop feedback mechanism (measurement, analysis, tuning) can be implemented as a separate module or service within NGHE Solution.
open_risks: Risk: If Vehicle mechanical efficiency is fundamentally limited (e.g., hydraulic power insufficient), no amount of Teleoperation or Safety Services tuning will achieve productivity_target.
Risk: If Teleoperation latency exceeds operator reaction time, remote operation may be unsafe or inefficient, forcing reliance on on-site operation only.
Risk: If Safety Services constraints are too conservative, they may prevent productivity_target achievement; if too permissive, they may allow unsafe conditions.
Risk: If the closed-loop feedback mechanism is not explicitly owned by NGHE Solution, it may be diffused across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services, leading to inconsistent or delayed tuning.
Risk: If NGHE Solution does not have clear authority over task allocation and mode switching (on-site vs. tele-operated), productivity optimization may be suboptimal.
evidence_needed: Vehicle mechanical efficiency data (cycle time, load capacity, power consumption) from design and testing.
Teleoperation latency and fidelity measurements from network and interface testing.
Safety Services constraint definitions and enforcement logic from safety analysis and policy review.
Productivity KPI measurement methodology and baseline data from representative operations trials.
Closed-loop feedback mechanism design and implementation plan for NGHE Solution.
Task allocation and mode switching logic design and implementation plan for NGHE Solution.
Interface definitions between Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services, including data flow, timing, and error handling.
